Text: What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
Options: Supervised uses GPUs while unsupervised does not;Supervised learning trains on labelled data while unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabelled data

Text: What does the bias-variance tradeoff describe?
Options: The tradeoff between model accuracy and speed;High bias leads to underfitting while high variance leads to overfitting — good models balance both
A search algorithm for graphs;An optimisation algorithm that iteratively adjusts model parameters in the direction of the negative gradient of the loss function

Text: What is the purpose of a sigmoid activation function?
Options: To introduce sparsity;To squash any input into a 0 to 1 range making it suitable for binary classification output layers

Text: What does regularisation do in machine learning?
Options: Increases model complexity;Adds a penalty to the loss function to reduce model complexity and prevent overfitting — L1 (Lasso) and L2 (Ridge) are common forms
A matrix used in neural network layers;A table showing true positives false positives true negatives and false negatives to evaluate classification model performance

Text: What is the purpose of a learning rate in training neural networks?
Options: The size of the dataset batch;Controls how large a step the optimiser takes when updating model weights — too high causes divergence too low causes slow convergence

Text: What does Principal Component Analysis (PCA) do?
Options: Classifies data points;Reduces dimensionality by projecting data onto orthogonal axes (principal components) that capture the most variance

Text: What is a decision tree in machine learning?
Options: A tree data structure for sorting;A model that splits data into subsets based on feature values at each node building a tree of decisions to predict an output
The accuracy of a model;The harmonic mean of precision and recall balancing both metrics — useful when class distribution is imbalanced

Text: What is the role of the softmax function in multi-class classification?
Options: To normalise inputs before training;To convert raw logits into a probability distribution over all classes where outputs sum to 1
Moving a model between servers;Using a pre-trained model on a large dataset and fine-tuning it on a smaller domain-specific dataset

Text: Write a Python function called 'normalize' that takes a list of numbers and returns a new list where each value is normalised to the range [0 1] using min-max normalisation.
Write a Python function called 'train_test_split_manual' that takes a list X and a float ratio (e.g. 0.8) and returns (X_train X_test) split at that ratio without shuffling.

Write a Python class called 'KNNClassifier' with fit(X_train y_train) and predict(X_test k) methods. Implement k-nearest neighbours using Euclidean distance. predict returns a list of predicted labels.
